origin.name: Kenya
origin.death_registration: Civil registrar at the district office
origin.investigating_authority: Kenya Police Service and government pathologist
origin.post_mortem_trigger: Unexpected, violent, unnatural, or suspicious deaths
origin.port_health_note: Port Health clearance is an additional mandatory step in Kenya. Required before export of human remains. Not required in most other countries.
origin.safari_transfer_note: Deaths in safari areas (Masai Mara, Amboseli, Tsavo, Laikipia) require road or charter aircraft transfer to Nairobi before any documentation can begin. Add significant time.
origin.embalming_note: Professional embalming available in Nairobi. Quality is limited outside the capital.
origin.key_document: Kenyan death certificate and Port Health clearance
origin.source: FCDO Kenya travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Nairobi guidance

destinations.name: United Kingdom
special_rules: Port Health clearance is mandatory and specific to Kenya. Cannot be bypassed.
Safari area deaths require transfer to Nairobi before documentation can begin. Add significant time.
Professional embalming limited to Nairobi. Remote area deaths require body transfer to the capital before preparation.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Kenyan documentation must be in certified English. The coroner for the district is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in Nairobi can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Kenya to UK Repatriation: Guidance for British Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Kenya. Repatriation to the UK takes 14-28 days. Port Health clearance, safari transfers, and Nairobi cargo all handled. Contact us 24/7.

destinations.name: Ireland

airlines.name: Kenya Airways
airlines.routes: NBO to DUB (via AMS or LHR)

special_rules: Irish Embassy in Nairobi handles consular matters.
Port Health clearance applies identically to Irish repatriations.
Safari area transfer to Nairobi required before any process can begin.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All documentation in certified English. The Coroner for the district is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000. The Irish Embassy in Nairobi can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Kenya to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Kenya and needs to come home to Ireland. 14-28 days. Port Health, safari transfers, and Nairobi cargo handled. Contact us 24/7.
